data.description:
## Motivation

Operators running parallel agents across many git worktrees (e.g., simultaneous C11-99 and C11-103 orchestrations on 2026-05-18) can't see at a glance which worktree a given pane is in. Today the operator has to either (a) ask the agent, (b) read the `agents.md` orchestration artifact, or (c) `c11 read-screen` and infer from prompts. This is friction that scales linearly with the number of in-flight delegators.

The underlying data is already captured. Each terminal surface has `cwd` (via OSC 7 from shell integration) and a `gitBranch` field on the session snapshot (with `isDirty`). What's missing is a rendering surface that turns "cwd + git context" into operator-glanceable signal in the sidebar.

## Design recommendation

Add two new canonical metadata keys, both **derived** (not agent-written):

| Key | Type | Renders as |
|---|---|---|
| `worktree` | string (basename or repo-relative path), ≤128 | sidebar monospace chip — shown only when in a *linked* worktree (not the main checkout) |
| `branch` | string, ≤64 | sidebar chip with git-style styling; shows `(detached @ abc1234)` for detached HEAD |

These join the existing canonical-keys family (`role`, `status`, `task`, `model`, `progress`, `terminal_type`) — same write path, same precedence model, same render-in-sidebar convention.

### Why this shape over alternatives

Five rendering options were weighed during the planning conversation (2026-05-18). Summary:

- **A. Auto-prefix the description** with "↳ worktree-name". Cheap, but conflates derived data with agent-authored prose; every agent writing a description must remember not to clobber the line. Architectural smell.
- **B. Title prefix.** Worst real-estate trade — title is already the most constrained surface (sidebar truncates ~25 chars) and competes with the `::` lineage convention.
- **C. New canonical sidebar chips** *(this proposal)*. Reuses existing canonical-keys infrastructure; agents don't have to write it; works for non-c11-aware processes (bash, vim, htop) since it's derived from cwd.
- **D. Per-worktree color hint** (hash-branch → color). Great peripheral signal, but ambiguous alone; works best as a *secondary* enhancement layered on top of C.
- **E. Group sidebar tabs by worktree** under collapsible headers. The strongest spatial signal, but the most ambitious UI surgery; defer until C has shipped and the operator has lived with it.

Recommendation: ship C first. D is a fast follow-up once the chips exist. E is a future ROADMAP entry.

## Implementation notes

### Data layer

- **Derive, don't store.** `worktree` and `branch` are computed projections of cwd. Do not write them to the surface manifest as `explicit` entries from inside c11 — store nothing, recompute on cwd change. Avoids drift if the agent `cd`s elsewhere and prevents the surface manifest from carrying state that doesn't belong to it.
- **Precedence:** introduce a new source tag, `derived`, ranked below `osc` in the existing `explicit > declare > osc > heuristic` chain. Agents may still override by writing `explicit` (rare; not the default path).
- **Where to compute:** the OSC 7 cwd-update handler is the trigger. After cwd lands, kick a background-queue job that runs `git rev-parse --show-superproject-working-tree`, `--show-toplevel`, and `git symbolic-ref --short HEAD` (or `git rev-parse --short HEAD` for detached). Update the rendered metadata when results arrive. Do NOT do this work on the main thread.

### Hot-path discipline (CRITICAL)

Per `code/c11/CLAUDE.md` "Typing-latency-sensitive paths":

- **Never run git resolution inside `TerminalSurface.forceRefresh()` or `WindowTerminalHostView.hitTest()`.** Both are per-keystroke or per-event.
- The OSC 7 handler fires once per prompt (not per keystroke), but even there: parse + dispatch off-main, do file I/O on a background queue, and only schedule the smallest possible main-actor mutation when the result lands.
- **Cache per cwd.** `(cwd, mtime-of-.git/HEAD)` is the cache key. Invalidate only on cwd change or coarse interval (e.g., 30s post-checkout to catch branch renames).

### Edge cases to test

| Case | Expected |
|---|---|
| cwd not in a git repo | omit both chips (don't render "worktree: —") |
| Main checkout, on a branch | show `branch` chip, no `worktree` chip |
| Linked worktree (`git worktree add ...`) | show both chips: `worktree: <basename>` + `branch: <branch>` |
| Submodule | resolve to superproject context (`--show-superproject-working-tree` first) |
| Detached HEAD | `branch` shows `(detached @ <short-sha>)` |
| Bare clone | omit both |
| Worktree pointing at a deleted branch | render `branch` empty / gracefully degrade |

### Schema + settings

- Update the canonical-keys table in `Sources/.../metadata...swift` (or wherever the renderer reads it) and `Resources/...skill...` reference docs.
- Add a `Settings → Sidebar → Show worktree/branch chips` toggle. **Default on, opt-out.** Operators not running multi-worktree workflows can hide.

## Test plan

Per c11 testing policy (`code/c11/CLAUDE.md` "Test quality policy"):

- Tests live in `c11LogicTests` (host-less, locally runnable).
- Tests verify the **resolution function's observable output** on a temp-dir-built git repo across the edge cases above. NOT source-code shape, NOT plist contents.
- Render-side test: a small SwiftUI snapshot or behavioral test confirming the chip appears/hides per the canonical-keys precedence (acceptable to defer if the snapshot infrastructure is heavy; a unit test against the `MetadataValue → ChipModel` projection function is enough).

## Out of scope (this ticket)

- Per-worktree color hint (option D) — separate follow-up ticket once C ships.
- Sidebar tab grouping by worktree (option E) — ROADMAP.
- Persistent storage of `worktree` / `branch` in workspace snapshots. Snapshots already capture `currentDirectory` + `gitBranch`; restored panes will re-derive on session resume.

## References

- Planning conversation: 2026-05-18, C11-103 Orchestrator session (operator atin + agent:claude-opus-4-7).
- Related: C11-103 (the in-flight session-persistence work that surfaced the parallel-worktree visibility problem).
- Existing canonical-keys infrastructure: `c11` skill's `references/metadata.md` and the surface manifest schema documented there.
data.title: Surface worktree + branch as derived canonical metadata chips in the sidebar

### Data layer

- **Derive, don't store.** `worktree` and `branch` are computed projections of cwd. Do not write them to the surface manifest as `explicit` entries from inside c11 — store nothing, recompute on cwd change. Avoids drift if the agent `cd`s elsewhere and prevents the surface manifest from carrying state that doesn't belong to it.
- **Precedence:** introduce a new source tag, `derived`, ranked below `osc` in the existing `explicit > declare > osc > heuristic` chain. Agents may still override by writing `explicit` (rare; not the default path).
- **Where to compute:** the OSC 7 cwd-update handler is the trigger. After cwd lands, kick a background-queue job that runs `git rev-parse --show-superproject-working-tree`, `--show-toplevel`, and `git symbolic-ref --short HEAD` (or `git rev-parse --short HEAD` for detached). Per `code/c11/CLAUDE.md` "Typing-latency-sensitive paths":

- **Never run git resolution inside `TerminalSurface.forceRefresh()` or `WindowTerminalHostView.hitTest()`.** Both are per-keystroke or per-event.

| Case | Expected |

- Existing canonical-keys infrastructure: `c11` skill's `references/metadata.md` and the surface manifest schema documented there.

---

## Refined SPEC + decisions (2026-05-18 Architect ↔ operator dialogue)

The Architect ran a four-round Planning Interview with the operator on 2026-05-18. This section records the resulting design decisions. **Where these conflict with the original ticket text above, these win** — the original "Design recommendation" / "Out of scope" sections were the input to the dialogue, not the output.

### Decisions

1. **Bundle Option C + Option D in this PR.** Ship the worktree+branch chips AND the per-worktree color hint together. The "Out of scope" entry for Option D in the original description is **superseded** — D is in scope for this ticket.
2. **Color hint placement:** colored dot prefix on the worktree chip (6–8pt circle before the chip text). NOT a background tint, NOT a left-edge bar.
3. **Color hash input:** absolute worktree path. Stable per-worktree regardless of branch state. Sibling worktrees (e.g., two parallel C11-104 worktrees, if that ever happened) would get different colors. Same worktree across sessions = same color.
4. **Branch chip in main checkout:** YES. The branch chip renders in main-checkout panes too (no worktree chip in that case, just branch). The "Main checkout, on a branch → show `branch` chip" row of the edge-case table stays as-written.
5. **Main-branch dimming:** when `branch ∈ {main, master, trunk}`, the branch chip renders with reduced opacity (15–25% — exact value the delegator's call, with operator review on PR). Feature branches render at full opacity. This applies to the branch chip everywhere it appears (main checkout AND inside a linked worktree).
6. **Worktree label format:** basename only. `code/c11-worktrees/c11-104-sidebar-chips` → `c11-104-sidebar-chips`. NOT repo-relative path. NOT "smart"-trimmed.
7. **Submodule context: stacked two rows.** When cwd is inside a submodule, render TWO chip rows:
   - Row 1 (primary): superproject's worktree+branch chips per the rules above.
   - Row 2 (indented or `↳`-prefixed): `submodule: <name>` + `branch: <name-or-detached>`.
   This **supersedes** the original "resolve to superproject context" edge-case row — both contexts are now rendered, with the superproject on top.
8. **Settings:** one master toggle, `Settings → Sidebar → Show worktree/branch chips`, default on. NOT split into per-chip or per-color sub-toggles. Granularity can be added in a follow-up if the unified toggle proves wrong.
9. **Test target:** `c11LogicTests` (host-less, locally runnable). Tests verify resolver output on temp-dir-built git repos and chip-projection behavior on constructed inputs. No source-grep tests, no plist-shape tests.

### Acceptance criteria (load-bearing for Phase 4)

The full per-criterion list with verification methods lives at `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/validation-plan.md`. Summarized here for the delegator's plan phase:

1. Resolver returns correct `worktree` (basename) + `branch` for a linked worktree (`git worktree add`).
2. Main checkout returns `worktree == nil`, `branch == <current>`.
3. Branch chip dims for branch ∈ {main, master, trunk}; not dimmed otherwise.
4. Linked-worktree chip carries colored-dot prefix; same absolute worktree path → same color across runs.
5. Detached HEAD renders as `(detached @ <short-sha>)`.
6. Submodule resolution returns BOTH superproject and submodule context; renderer produces two rows.
7. Not-in-git, bare clone, and worktree-with-deleted-branch all degrade gracefully without crashes.
8. Resolver runs entirely off the main thread; OSC 7 handler enqueues, does not block.
9. `TerminalSurface.forceRefresh()` and `WindowTerminalHostView.hitTest()` bodies are NOT modified to do new git/IO work.
10. Cache invalidates on cwd change AND on `.git/HEAD` mtime change.
11. New `derived` precedence tag exists between `osc` and `heuristic`; `osc` value wins over `derived` for the same key.
12. Settings toggle exists, default on, live-toggleable, gates chip rendering.
13. Tests live in `c11LogicTests`, not `c11Tests`.
14. No source-grep / source-shape tests.
15. `c11` skill's `references/metadata.md` updated for the two new keys + the new precedence tier.

### Run config

- **Autonomy:** Fully Autonomous delegator. Routes around everything short of spend > $20 or permanent data destruction.
- **Concurrent delegator cap:** N=1 (single-ticket run).
- **Master Validator:** off.
- **Result Validator:** **on** at Phase 4 — fresh-eyes audit warranted by the typing-latency hot-path discipline.
- **Closeout audit:** on (default).
- **Ticket fidelity:** Verbose (this section).
- **Phase 3 handoff:** the Architect pane (surface:15 "C11-104 Arch") promotes to Orchestrator after Phase 2 artifacts land. No fresh Orchestrator pane spawned.

### Worktree convention for the delegator

- Path: `code/c11-worktrees/c11-104-sidebar-chips`
- Branch: `feat/c11-104-sidebar-chips`
- Anchor: `origin/main` at the SHA current when the delegator spawns.

### Orchestration artifacts

- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/run-state.md` — configuration + implementation plan + decision log.
- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/agents.md` — role roster + workspace pane refs + event log.
- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/validation-plan.md` — per-AC verification rubric (Result Validator reads this).

provenance.reason: Phase 1 Planning Interview + Phase 2 decisions appended
Phase 1 Planning Interview complete; refined SPEC appended; Phase 2 artifacts on disk
Step 2 of trident-pane handoff prompt; merged plan-review artifact

data.body:
## Trident plan-review for C11-104 — done

**Verdict:** revise-then-proceed (plan-level FAIL)

**Artifact:** `art_01KRZ23GBH40S85V74CW09PDTD` (`synthesis-action.md`)
**Full review pack:** `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/c11-104-plan-review-pack-2026-05-18T2228/`
**Coverage:** 6 of 9 reviewers succeeded. Gemini was rate-limited (Google API "exhausted capacity on this model" across all 3 lenses). Consensus thresholds in the action synthesis are taken across the 6 successful reviews (2 models × 3 lenses).

The architecture survives this pass intact. None of the validated findings demand re-architecture; they demand an integration map, a few correctness fixes, a settled `derived`-source policy, and path corrections. After those land the plan is executable.

### Highest-confidence findings (cross-lens, cross-model)

1. **Missing integration map for existing c11 git infrastructure** (B1). The plan never names `TabManager.initialWorkspaceGitMetadataSnapshot`, `panelGitBranches`/`panelDirectories`, the existing "Branch + Directory" sidebar row, or the `sidebarShowBranchDirectory` Settings toggle. Implemented literally, the delegator builds a parallel git pipeline, a sidebar that shows branch info twice, and a Settings panel with two near-identical default-on toggles. **Operator decision needed:** replace vs. extend vs. coexist.
2. **`(cwd, mtime(.git/HEAD))` cache key is wrong for linked worktrees and submodules** (B2) — the exact cases the ticket exists for. In a linked worktree, `<wt>/.git` is a file, and the real HEAD lives at `<common-dir>/worktrees/<name>/HEAD`. Resolver must invalidate against the *git-resolved* HEAD path.
3. **Submodule resolution sequence is mechanically wrong** (B3). `--show-toplevel` from a submodule returns the *submodule* root, not the superproject. Stacked-two-row decision requires a two-pass algorithm with cwd set to the superproject for the outer row.
4. **No stale-async-result handling** (B4). Concurrent OSC 7 fires can apply a late result for cwd A on top of fresh chips for cwd C. Reuse the generation-token + expected-cwd pattern already in `TabManager.scheduleInitialWorkspaceGitMetadataRefresh`.
5. **`derived`-source policy undefined** (B5) — socket writability, snapshot persistence, and clear semantics all left implicit. Without a written policy, agents can write `source=derived` over the socket, chips persist in workspace snapshots, and `cd ~/Desktop` leaves stale chips.
6. **Wrong file paths in BUILDPLAN/ACs** (B6) — `Tests/c11LogicTests/` and `Sources/Sidebar/` don't exist. c11 uses `c11Tests/` with target membership; chip code lives in `Sources/AgentChip.swift` + `Sources/ContentView.swift`.

### Triage per Lattice Review Verdict Routing

Per Step 3 of the trident-pane handoff: route every finding to Obvious / Evolutionary / Complex.

**Obvious (technical fixes the planner can apply mechanically):**
- B3 (submodule two-pass algorithm — semantics-determined)
- B4 (reuse existing generation-token pattern)
- B6 (path corrections)
- I1 (localization clauses + xcstrings)
- I2 (AC10 reworded with `dispatchPrecondition`)
- I3 (`git` subprocess timeout policy)
- I7 (branch length: no resolver-side limit, view-layer truncate)
- M1–M3, M5–M6 (mechanical reword/tighten)
- EW1 (worktree color → surface-model property)

**Complex (real design questions — see synthesis-action.md "Surface to user" and S-rows below):**
- B1 — integration map: replace vs. extend vs. coexist (operator's call). Drives S2, M4, and the existing-row regression policy.
- B2 — cache-key algorithm: resolved-HEAD mtime vs. FSEvents (E2). FSEvents fixes I6 (stale-worktree) too.
- B5 — `derived` policy: socket writability, snapshot persistence, clear semantics (three operator calls).
- I4 — workspace multi-surface aggregation rule.
- I5 — restore-window pre-warm strategy.
- I6 — stale-worktree behavior.
- M2 — `TabItemView` `Equatable`/body-skip preservation (technical but high-stakes per CLAUDE.md typing-latency policy).
- M4 — dirty-marker + PR-pill regression policy (tied to B1).
- S1 (C+D bundle), S3 (palette + contrast), S4 (agent-readable derived chips), S5 (submodule chip verb/name), S7 (perf budget + observability), S8 (**OSC 7 doesn't fire inside TUI agent panes** — primary-use-case correctness concern), S9 (rollback policy for AC20), S10 (pre-merge tagged-build smoke).

**Evolutionary (skipped — see separate skip comments below):**
- S6, E1, E2, E3.

### Most load-bearing complex finding to call out

**S8 — OSC 7 doesn't fire inside TUI agent panes.** Adversarial-Claude argues that OSC 7 is shell-mediated, so inside agent panes (`claude`/`codex`/`kimi` consuming keystrokes without a shell) the resolver never fires — and those panes are the motivation section's *primary* use case. If true on c11's stack, the feature partially fails its main use case. The integration map (B1) decision is downstream: if S8 is real, B1 likely needs to lean on c11's existing socket telemetry (`report_pwd`/`report_git_branch`) rather than OSC 7 alone. **Operator/architect should resolve S8 before B1.**

### Reviewer-pack metadata

- 6 individual reviews: Standard (Claude+Codex), Adversarial (Claude+Codex), Evolutionary (Claude+Codex). Sizes 12K–32K each.
- 4 syntheses: lens-standard, lens-adversarial, lens-evolutionary, action-ready (the attached artifact).
- All 10 files in `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/c11-104-plan-review-pack-2026-05-18T2228/`.

### Status routing

Moving C11-104 → `in_planning`. The Architect/Orchestrator picks up `synthesis-action.md`, decides on the complex items (especially S8 then B1), and revises the plan (run-state.md / validation-plan.md / refined-SPEC section of the ticket). After revision, re-spin trident or proceed to delegation per the operator's call.

Skipping evolutionary finding S6 (worktree chips on non-terminal surfaces — markdown/browser): scope-creep at the plan level; deferred per synthesis-action.md. The current plan correctly out-of-scopes this. Worth knowing the data-layer decision (EW1: worktree color on surface model not chip view) interacts with it, which is already captured as EW1.

Skipping evolutionary finding E1 (reframe PR as 'ship the derived precedence tier and derivation pipeline, with GitContextDeriver as first implementation'): strong cross-lens evolutionary signal but expands C11-104's scope from 'ship two chips' to 'ship a derivation framework'. That is the plan author's bet to make, not the trident pane's call. Captured in synthesis-action.md E1; surface for operator decision during planning revision.

Skipping evolutionary finding E2 (FSEvents-based cache invalidation on resolved HEAD path): closes two real correctness gaps (Tower/Fork/source-tree branch switches; stale-worktree-removed case I6) with modest cost, but is a design upgrade rather than a fix-the-known-bug edit. Operator's call during planning revision. See synthesis-action.md E2.

Skipping evolutionary finding E3 (file a follow-up Lattice ticket now for a second deriver — host/SSH target, container, kubectl — to land within 1–2 weeks of C11-104 as a forcing function on the abstraction shape): only relevant if E1 is adopted. Plan author may or may not want the commitment. See synthesis-action.md E3.

provenance.reason: Trident plan-review verdict: revise-then-proceed (plan-level FAIL). Lattice does not allow direct in_progress → in_planning transition; routing to needs_human per Step 4 of trident-pane handoff prompt (multiple complex findings need operator input: B1 integration map, B5 derived-source policy, S8 OSC 7 fundamental). Architect/Orchestrator: read art_01KRZ23GBH40S85V74CW09PDTD (synthesis-action.md), make complex calls, revise plan in run-state.md / validation-plan.md / refined-SPEC. Then re-enter in_planning to resume.
PR opened at https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11/pull/181; ready for Result Validator.

data.from:
## Motivation

Operators running parallel agents across many git worktrees (e.g., simultaneous C11-99 and C11-103 orchestrations on 2026-05-18) can't see at a glance which worktree a given pane is in. Today the operator has to either (a) ask the agent, (b) read the `agents.md` orchestration artifact, or (c) `c11 read-screen` and infer from prompts. This is friction that scales linearly with the number of in-flight delegators.

The underlying data is already captured. Each terminal surface has `cwd` (via OSC 7 from shell integration) and a `gitBranch` field on the session snapshot (with `isDirty`). What's missing is a rendering surface that turns "cwd + git context" into operator-glanceable signal in the sidebar.

## Design recommendation

Add two new canonical metadata keys, both **derived** (not agent-written):

| Key | Type | Renders as |
|---|---|---|
| `worktree` | string (basename or repo-relative path), ≤128 | sidebar monospace chip — shown only when in a *linked* worktree (not the main checkout) |
| `branch` | string, ≤64 | sidebar chip with git-style styling; shows `(detached @ abc1234)` for detached HEAD |

These join the existing canonical-keys family (`role`, `status`, `task`, `model`, `progress`, `terminal_type`) — same write path, same precedence model, same render-in-sidebar convention.

### Why this shape over alternatives

Five rendering options were weighed during the planning conversation (2026-05-18). Summary:

- **A. Auto-prefix the description** with "↳ worktree-name". Cheap, but conflates derived data with agent-authored prose; every agent writing a description must remember not to clobber the line. Architectural smell.
- **B. Title prefix.** Worst real-estate trade — title is already the most constrained surface (sidebar truncates ~25 chars) and competes with the `::` lineage convention.
- **C. New canonical sidebar chips** *(this proposal)*. Reuses existing canonical-keys infrastructure; agents don't have to write it; works for non-c11-aware processes (bash, vim, htop) since it's derived from cwd.
- **D. Per-worktree color hint** (hash-branch → color). Great peripheral signal, but ambiguous alone; works best as a *secondary* enhancement layered on top of C.
- **E. Group sidebar tabs by worktree** under collapsible headers. The strongest spatial signal, but the most ambitious UI surgery; defer until C has shipped and the operator has lived with it.

Recommendation: ship C first. D is a fast follow-up once the chips exist. E is a future ROADMAP entry.

## Implementation notes

### Data layer

- **Derive, don't store.** `worktree` and `branch` are computed projections of cwd. Do not write them to the surface manifest as `explicit` entries from inside c11 — store nothing, recompute on cwd change. Avoids drift if the agent `cd`s elsewhere and prevents the surface manifest from carrying state that doesn't belong to it.
- **Precedence:** introduce a new source tag, `derived`, ranked below `osc` in the existing `explicit > declare > osc > heuristic` chain. Agents may still override by writing `explicit` (rare; not the default path).
- **Where to compute:** the OSC 7 cwd-update handler is the trigger. After cwd lands, kick a background-queue job that runs `git rev-parse --show-superproject-working-tree`, `--show-toplevel`, and `git symbolic-ref --short HEAD` (or `git rev-parse --short HEAD` for detached). Update the rendered metadata when results arrive. Do NOT do this work on the main thread.

### Hot-path discipline (CRITICAL)

Per `code/c11/CLAUDE.md` "Typing-latency-sensitive paths":

- **Never run git resolution inside `TerminalSurface.forceRefresh()` or `WindowTerminalHostView.hitTest()`.** Both are per-keystroke or per-event.
- The OSC 7 handler fires once per prompt (not per keystroke), but even there: parse + dispatch off-main, do file I/O on a background queue, and only schedule the smallest possible main-actor mutation when the result lands.
- **Cache per cwd.** `(cwd, mtime-of-.git/HEAD)` is the cache key. Invalidate only on cwd change or coarse interval (e.g., 30s post-checkout to catch branch renames).

### Edge cases to test

| Case | Expected |
|---|---|
| cwd not in a git repo | omit both chips (don't render "worktree: —") |
| Main checkout, on a branch | show `branch` chip, no `worktree` chip |
| Linked worktree (`git worktree add ...`) | show both chips: `worktree: <basename>` + `branch: <branch>` |
| Submodule | resolve to superproject context (`--show-superproject-working-tree` first) |
| Detached HEAD | `branch` shows `(detached @ <short-sha>)` |
| Bare clone | omit both |
| Worktree pointing at a deleted branch | render `branch` empty / gracefully degrade |

### Schema + settings

- Update the canonical-keys table in `Sources/.../metadata...swift` (or wherever the renderer reads it) and `Resources/...skill...` reference docs.
- Add a `Settings → Sidebar → Show worktree/branch chips` toggle. **Default on, opt-out.** Operators not running multi-worktree workflows can hide.

## Test plan

Per c11 testing policy (`code/c11/CLAUDE.md` "Test quality policy"):

- Tests live in `c11LogicTests` (host-less, locally runnable).
- Tests verify the **resolution function's observable output** on a temp-dir-built git repo across the edge cases above. NOT source-code shape, NOT plist contents.
- Render-side test: a small SwiftUI snapshot or behavioral test confirming the chip appears/hides per the canonical-keys precedence (acceptable to defer if the snapshot infrastructure is heavy; a unit test against the `MetadataValue → ChipModel` projection function is enough).

## Out of scope (this ticket)

- Per-worktree color hint (option D) — separate follow-up ticket once C ships.
- Sidebar tab grouping by worktree (option E) — ROADMAP.
- Persistent storage of `worktree` / `branch` in workspace snapshots. Snapshots already capture `currentDirectory` + `gitBranch`; restored panes will re-derive on session resume.

## References

- Planning conversation: 2026-05-18, C11-103 Orchestrator session (operator atin + agent:claude-opus-4-7).
- Related: C11-103 (the in-flight session-persistence work that surfaced the parallel-worktree visibility problem).
- Existing canonical-keys infrastructure: `c11` skill's `references/metadata.md` and the surface manifest schema documented there.

---

## Refined SPEC + decisions (2026-05-18 Architect ↔ operator dialogue)

The Architect ran a four-round Planning Interview with the operator on 2026-05-18. This section records the resulting design decisions. **Where these conflict with the original ticket text above, these win** — the original "Design recommendation" / "Out of scope" sections were the input to the dialogue, not the output.

### Decisions

1. **Bundle Option C + Option D in this PR.** Ship the worktree+branch chips AND the per-worktree color hint together. The "Out of scope" entry for Option D in the original description is **superseded** — D is in scope for this ticket.
2. **Color hint placement:** colored dot prefix on the worktree chip (6–8pt circle before the chip text). NOT a background tint, NOT a left-edge bar.
3. **Color hash input:** absolute worktree path. Stable per-worktree regardless of branch state. Sibling worktrees (e.g., two parallel C11-104 worktrees, if that ever happened) would get different colors. Same worktree across sessions = same color.
4. **Branch chip in main checkout:** YES. The branch chip renders in main-checkout panes too (no worktree chip in that case, just branch). The "Main checkout, on a branch → show `branch` chip" row of the edge-case table stays as-written.
5. **Main-branch dimming:** when `branch ∈ {main, master, trunk}`, the branch chip renders with reduced opacity (15–25% — exact value the delegator's call, with operator review on PR). Feature branches render at full opacity. This applies to the branch chip everywhere it appears (main checkout AND inside a linked worktree).
6. **Worktree label format:** basename only. `code/c11-worktrees/c11-104-sidebar-chips` → `c11-104-sidebar-chips`. NOT repo-relative path. NOT "smart"-trimmed.
7. **Submodule context: stacked two rows.** When cwd is inside a submodule, render TWO chip rows:
   - Row 1 (primary): superproject's worktree+branch chips per the rules above.
   - Row 2 (indented or `↳`-prefixed): `submodule: <name>` + `branch: <name-or-detached>`.
   This **supersedes** the original "resolve to superproject context" edge-case row — both contexts are now rendered, with the superproject on top.
8. **Settings:** one master toggle, `Settings → Sidebar → Show worktree/branch chips`, default on. NOT split into per-chip or per-color sub-toggles. Granularity can be added in a follow-up if the unified toggle proves wrong.
9. **Test target:** `c11LogicTests` (host-less, locally runnable). Tests verify resolver output on temp-dir-built git repos and chip-projection behavior on constructed inputs. No source-grep tests, no plist-shape tests.

### Acceptance criteria (load-bearing for Phase 4)

The full per-criterion list with verification methods lives at `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/validation-plan.md`. Summarized here for the delegator's plan phase:

1. Resolver returns correct `worktree` (basename) + `branch` for a linked worktree (`git worktree add`).
2. Main checkout returns `worktree == nil`, `branch == <current>`.
3. Branch chip dims for branch ∈ {main, master, trunk}; not dimmed otherwise.
4. Linked-worktree chip carries colored-dot prefix; same absolute worktree path → same color across runs.
5. Detached HEAD renders as `(detached @ <short-sha>)`.
6. Submodule resolution returns BOTH superproject and submodule context; renderer produces two rows.
7. Not-in-git, bare clone, and worktree-with-deleted-branch all degrade gracefully without crashes.
8. Resolver runs entirely off the main thread; OSC 7 handler enqueues, does not block.
9. `TerminalSurface.forceRefresh()` and `WindowTerminalHostView.hitTest()` bodies are NOT modified to do new git/IO work.
10. Cache invalidates on cwd change AND on `.git/HEAD` mtime change.
11. New `derived` precedence tag exists between `osc` and `heuristic`; `osc` value wins over `derived` for the same key.
12. Settings toggle exists, default on, live-toggleable, gates chip rendering.
13. Tests live in `c11LogicTests`, not `c11Tests`.
14. No source-grep / source-shape tests.
15. `c11` skill's `references/metadata.md` updated for the two new keys + the new precedence tier.

### Run config

- **Autonomy:** Fully Autonomous delegator. Routes around everything short of spend > $20 or permanent data destruction.
- **Concurrent delegator cap:** N=1 (single-ticket run).
- **Master Validator:** off.
- **Result Validator:** **on** at Phase 4 — fresh-eyes audit warranted by the typing-latency hot-path discipline.
- **Closeout audit:** on (default).
- **Ticket fidelity:** Verbose (this section).
- **Phase 3 handoff:** the Architect pane (surface:15 "C11-104 Arch") promotes to Orchestrator after Phase 2 artifacts land. No fresh Orchestrator pane spawned.

### Worktree convention for the delegator

- Path: `code/c11-worktrees/c11-104-sidebar-chips`
- Branch: `feat/c11-104-sidebar-chips`
- Anchor: `origin/main` at the SHA current when the delegator spawns.

### Orchestration artifacts

- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/run-state.md` — configuration + implementation plan + decision log.
- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/agents.md` — role roster + workspace pane refs + event log.
- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/validation-plan.md` — per-AC verification rubric (Result Validator reads this).

The full per-criterion list with verification methods lives at `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/validation-plan.md`. Summarized here for the delegator's plan phase:

1. Resolver returns correct `worktree` (basename) + `branch` for a linked worktree (`git worktree add`).
2. Main checkout returns `worktree == nil`, `branch == <current>`.
3. Branch chip dims for branch ∈ {main, master, trunk}; not dimmed otherwise.
4. Linked-worktree chip carries colored-dot prefix; same absolute worktree path → same color across runs.
5. Detached HEAD renders as `(detached @ <short-sha>)`.
6. Submodule resolution returns BOTH superproject and submodule context; renderer produces two rows.
7. Not-in-git, bare clone, and worktree-with-deleted-branch all degrade gracefully without crashes.
8. Resolver runs entirely off the main thread; OSC 7 handler enqueues, does not block.
9. `TerminalSurface.forceRefresh()` and `WindowTerminalHostView.hitTest()` bodies are NOT modified to do new git/IO work.

- **Concurrent delegator cap:** N=1 (single-ticket run).

### Worktree convention for the delegator

### Orchestration artifacts

- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/agents.md` — role roster + workspace pane refs + event log.
- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/validation-plan.md` — per-AC verification rubric (Result Validator reads this).

---

## Refined SPEC + decisions **v2 (2026-05-19, post-trident-review)**

The trident plan-review fired against v1 (`art_01KRZ23GBH40S85V74CW09PDTD`) returned **`revise-then-proceed`**: six blocker-level gaps (B1–B6), seven important items (I1–I7), six mechanical (M1–M6), one evolutionary clear win (EW1), ten operator-call items (S1–S10), three evolutionary signals (E1–E3). The architecture survives; the plan needed substantial revision.

**v2 supersedes v1.** Where this section conflicts with the v1 "Refined SPEC + decisions" or the original ticket text, **v2 wins**.

### Empirical findings during v2 revision

- **OSC 7 is not the trigger.** c11 doesn't emit or consume OSC 7. The trigger is `report_pwd` sent over c11's socket by zsh/bash `precmd` hooks (`Resources/shell-integration/`). Inside agent panes (claude/codex/kimi) the precmd hook never fires, so `report_pwd` doesn't fire. **But:** the **initial workspace git probe** runs on workspace setup against `panelDirectories[surfaceId]` (seeded with the panel's spawn-time cwd), which IS the worktree. **Primary use case works at startup.** Stale-on-internal-`cd` is a documented limitation.
- **Existing infrastructure to extend, not build alongside.** Surveyed at file:line precision (see `run-state.md` "Existing infrastructure to extend"). Load-bearing:
  - `Workspace.panelGitBranches: [UUID: SidebarGitBranchState]` — per-surface branch + isDirty (`Sources/Workspace.swift:5297`)
  - `Workspace.panelDirectories: [UUID: String]` — per-surface cwd (`Sources/Workspace.swift:5274`)
  - `TabManager.scheduleInitialWorkspaceGitMetadataRefresh()` — probe trigger with 6-attempt retry (`Sources/TabManager.swift:1509-1521`)
  - `TabManager.applyWorkspaceGitMetadataSnapshot()` — result-handler with generation token + expected-cwd guards (`Sources/TabManager.swift:1589-1641`) — **this is the B4 stale-async-result pattern v2 reuses**
  - `verticalBranchDirectoryLines()` — existing sidebar text render (`Sources/ContentView.swift:12364-12390`) — **v2 replaces**
  - `@AppStorage("sidebarShowBranchDirectory")` — Settings toggle (`Sources/c11App.swift:4435`) — **v2 preserves key, renames UI label**

### Operator decisions (v2)

| ID | Decision |
|---|---|
| **S2 integration** | **Replace** the existing branch+directory row with the new chips. Extend `TabManager`'s probe to populate richer state. Preserve `@AppStorage("sidebarShowBranchDirectory")` key; rename UI label only. |
| **S1 C+D bundle** | Stays bundled (operator already chose this; trident reviewer's challenge noted but not adopted). |
| **S3 palette** | Muted/desaturated 10-color palette aligned with c11 theme accents; ThemeKey-backed entries for Light/Dark slots. Operator can tune via theme JSON. |
| **S4 agent-readable** | **Yes** via `c11 get-metadata --key worktree` and `--key branch`. Socket writes with `source=derived` from external clients rejected with `invalid_source`. |
| **B5 derived lifecycle** | Internal-only writes; excluded from snapshot capture; resolver issues explicit clear when not-in-repo. |
| **E1 reframe** | **Yes**: introduce `MetadataDeriver` protocol + `DerivationCoordinator` + `GitContext` value type. `GitContextDeriver` is the first concrete implementation. |
| **S5 submodule label** | `submodule: <name>` with name source = `.gitmodules` configured name → path-from-superproject-root → basename. |
| **S7 perf budget** | No AC-level perf budget; delegator may add logging/counters at its discretion. Operator accepts the anecdotal-feedback risk. |
| **S9 rollback policy** | If post-merge typing-latency regression surfaces, flip `sidebarShowBranchDirectory` default to off in nightly via a follow-up commit; full PR revert is the fallback. Documented in PR body (P7). |
| **S10 pre-merge smoke** | Defer. Computer-use harness doesn't exist yet. Post-merge smoke via tagged build is acceptable for v1. |

### Architecture (v2)

1. **New derivation seam (E1):**
   - `protocol MetadataDeriver` — uniform interface; `func derive(for: Surface, cwd: URL) async throws -> [DerivedKV]`.
   - `DerivationCoordinator` — runs derivers on `TabManager.initialWorkspaceGitProbeQueue` (existing off-main queue at `Sources/TabManager.swift:1008`); applies results via generation-token + expected-cwd guard (existing pattern); caches per-`(surface, key)` against `(cwd, resolvedHeadMtime)`.
   - `GitContext` value type — `{ worktreeRoot: URL?, worktreeKind: .main|.linked|.bare|.notInRepo|.stale, branch: BranchState (.named(String)|.detached(String)|.noBranch), submodule: SubmoduleContext?, colorSeed: String?, isDirty: Bool }`.
   - `GitContextDeriver: MetadataDeriver` — wraps the work currently in `TabManager.initialWorkspaceGitMetadataSnapshot()` plus the new linked-worktree + submodule detection. Returns a `GitContext`.

2. **Existing `TabManager` probe extends to call the coordinator** rather than its own ad-hoc invocation. The legacy `SidebarGitBranchState` (`{branch, isDirty}`) becomes a projection over `GitContext`; the coordinator writes both the new derived metadata keys AND maintains the legacy struct for backward compatibility with existing consumers (per P6).

3. **Sidebar chip render replaces `verticalBranchDirectoryLines()`** (per S2 replace). Chip-shaped output with the render rules in `run-state.md`'s render-rules table.

4. **`derived` source policy** (per B5): socket writes from external clients rejected; snapshot capture excludes; resolver explicit-clears on `.notInRepo` / `.stale`.

### Critical correctness items (B2–B6 fixed in v2)

- **B2 cache key**: invalidates against `git rev-parse --git-path HEAD` (the *resolved* HEAD), not `<cwd>/.git/HEAD`. Works correctly for linked worktrees + submodules.
- **B3 submodule two-pass**: detect via `--show-superproject-working-tree`; run separate full resolution against the superproject path for the outer row, against original cwd for the inner row. Display-name source per S5 fallback chain.
- **B4 stale-async**: reuse the existing `TabManager` gen-token + expected-cwd pattern via `DerivationCoordinator`.
- **B5 derived policy**: see Operator Decisions above.
- **B6 file paths**: tests in `c11Tests/` (not `Tests/c11LogicTests/`) with `c11LogicTests` target membership. Chip render extends `Sources/AgentChip.swift` + `Sources/ContentView.swift` in place. Coordinator/deriver code lives under `Sources/` (delegator picks exact filename; if `Sources/Metadata/` doesn't yet exist, follow flat convention).

### Important items folded in (I1–I7)

- **I1 localization**: every new user-facing string via `String(localized:defaultValue:)` against `Resources/Localizable.xcstrings`. Translator sub-agent spawn for ja/uk/ko/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/ru after English is final.
- **I2 hot-path runtime check**: `dispatchPrecondition(.notOnQueue(.main))` at deriver + coordinator entry points; runtime crash if accidentally invoked on main.
- **I3 git timeout**: 2s per `git` invocation; on timeout return `.timeout` state, chip clears.
- **I4 multi-surface render policy**: per-surface state, dedup'd across surfaces sharing the same `(worktreeRoot, branch)`. Extend `orderedUniqueBranchDirectoryEntries()` dedup logic.
- **I5 restore-window**: on workspace restore, paint snapshotted `gitBranch` value as the chip's initial render while the fresh deriver runs in the background. Avoids "30-surface chips paint one-by-one" failure mode.
- **I6 stale-worktree**: deriver detects cwd-missing / resolved-HEAD-missing → returns `.stale`. Chip clears (no strikethrough).
- **I7 branch-length**: no resolver-side limit; view-layer middle-ellipsis truncate. Full value queryable via `c11 get-metadata`.

### Mechanical items (M1–M6)

- M1 deterministic color-hash test inputs.
- M2 hot-path audit extends to `Sources/ContentView.swift` `TabItemView`.
- M3 drop "30s coarse interval"; cache invalidates on cwd change + resolved-HEAD mtime change only.
- M4 dirty marker preserved as branch-chip suffix `*`; PR pill render path unchanged.
- M5 color hash input = `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` (real path, symlinks resolved).
- M6 dimming = ThemeKey-backed opacity, defaults `chipTextDimmedOpacityLight = 0.65`, `chipTextDimmedOpacityDark = 0.55`.

### Evolutionary clear win (EW1)

`worktreeColor: Color?` stored on `GitContext` (and via the coordinator, on `panelGitBranches`'s replacement state), not inside the chip view. Future consumers (Option E, command-palette jump-to-worktree, etc.) read the same value.

### Acceptance criteria (v2)

Full list with verification methods at `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/validation-plan.md`. 30 ACs total (AC1–AC30 pre-merge-static, AC31–AC33 post-merge-smoke) + 7 PR-level checks. Major changes from v1:

- Tests in `c11Tests/` with `c11LogicTests` target membership (not `Tests/c11LogicTests/`).
- Cache test against the **resolved HEAD path** (linked-worktree + submodule fixtures).
- Stale-async via gen-token + expected-cwd guard.
- Runtime `dispatchPrecondition` instead of grep-shaped "no git on main."
- Socket-write-rejection AC for `source=derived`.
- Socket-read AC for `get-metadata --key worktree`/`branch`.
- Snapshot-exclude AC.
- Restore-warmpath AC.
- "No duplicate render path" AC — confirms `verticalBranchDirectoryLines()` is replaced, not parallel-running.
- `@AppStorage` key preservation AC.
- Localization AC for all six non-English locales.

### Out of scope (v2 — narrower than v1)

- Option E (sidebar tab grouping) — ROADMAP.
- Worktree chips on non-terminal surfaces — S6 deferred.
- FSEvents-based cache invalidation (E2) — follow-up if mtime proves insufficient.
- A second `MetadataDeriver` (host/SSH, container, kubectl, AWS) — E3 deferred; abstraction is ready.
- Pre-merge tagged-build smoke gate (S10) — depends on `c11-computer-use` infra that doesn't yet exist.
- Formal perf-budget AC (S7) — delegator may log/count at discretion; no AC gate.
- Solving the agent-pane stale-on-internal-`cd` case — primary use case works via initial probe; internal `cd` after agent spawn is a follow-up if it bites.

### Run config (unchanged from v1)

- **Autonomy:** Fully Autonomous delegator
- **N:** 1
- **Master Validator:** off
- **Result Validator:** **on** (Phase 4)
- **Closeout audit:** on
- **Phase 3 handoff:** Architect pane promotes back to Orchestrator after v2 ships to a fresh delegator (the v1 delegator's context is contaminated; v1 surface:16 closes, new delegator surface spawns)

### Worktree convention (unchanged from v1)

- Path: `code/c11-worktrees/c11-104-sidebar-chips` (already exists from v1; delegator inherits)
- Branch: `feat/c11-104-sidebar-chips` (already exists from v1; delegator inherits)
- Anchor: `origin/main` at `7cbc27d31` (the v1 anchor; v2 reuses)

### Orchestration artifacts (v2)

- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/run-state.md` — v2 source of truth.
- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/agents.md` — role roster (updates as v2 delegator spawns).
- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/validation-plan.md` — 30 ACs + 7 PR-level checks.
- `.lattice/orchestration/c11-104/c11-104-plan-review-pack-2026-05-18T2228/` — the trident pack that produced this revision (synthesis, six reviews, lens syntheses).

data.body:
## v2 plan response to trident review (art_01KRZ23GBH40S85V74CW09PDTD)

Trident verdict was `revise-then-proceed`. The Architect (this session) ran v2 revision today.

**Empirical findings during revision**
- **OSC 7 doesn't trigger anything in c11.** Trigger is `report_pwd` socket from zsh/bash precmd. Inside agent panes precmd doesn't fire — primary use case is rescued by the **initial workspace git probe** which runs against `panelDirectories[surfaceId]` (seeded at panel spawn = the worktree). Chips will derive correctly at agent startup; internal-`cd`-while-agent-runs is a documented limitation.
- **Existing infrastructure to extend, not duplicate.** `TabManager.scheduleInitialWorkspaceGitMetadataRefresh` + `panelGitBranches` + `panelDirectories` + `@AppStorage('sidebarShowBranchDirectory')` form the system v2 extends. Generation-token + expected-cwd guard pattern reused for B4.

**Operator decisions captured**
- S2: replace existing branch+directory row with chips. `@AppStorage` key preserved.
- S1: stay bundled (C+D).
- S3: muted/desaturated palette, ThemeKey-backed Light/Dark.
- S4: derived chips readable via socket; writes rejected.
- B5: internal-only writes; excluded from snapshots; resolver clears on not-in-repo.
- E1: yes — introduce `MetadataDeriver` + `DerivationCoordinator` + `GitContext` value type.

**v2 artifacts on disk**
- Refined SPEC v2 section appended to this ticket's description.
- `run-state.md` rewritten with v2 implementation plan + file:line map of existing infrastructure.
- `validation-plan.md` rewritten with 30 ACs covering all B/I/M/S/E points.

**Pending**: kill v1 delegator (surface:16, context contaminated by v1 spec), transition status `needs_human → planned`, spawn v2 delegator with new boot prompt.

provenance.reason: PR #181 needs v2 amendments (retire legacy row, merge toggles, add MetadataDeriver protocol); v1 delegator to amend in place

v2 amendments pushed: retired legacy row, merged toggles, added MetadataDeriver+DerivationCoordinator seam, tightened derived policy + submodule-name fallback + localization. 48 C11-104 tests green locally.
Phase 4 Result Validator audit of PR #181 against the 30-AC validation plan; merge gate verdict: clear (with follow-up C11-106)
PR #181 merged at 2b1be4220 on 2026-05-19T13:12:06Z; Phase 4 validation report attached as review-role artifact; merge gate clear; follow-up captured as C11-106.
